claim_status: No-claim security incident and patch rehearsal record template only; no executed private-intake tabletop, no emergency patch dry run, no regression or backport evidence, no signing or update dry-run evidence, no coordinated disclosure rehearsal, no postmortem evidence, no role matrix review, no backup-owner coverage, no owner review, no production-safe browsing claim, no supported security versions claim, no incident-response readiness claim, no emergency patch capacity claim, no disclosure authority claim, no stable promotion authority claim, no signing authority claim, no incident closure authority claim, and no implementation claim.

rehearsal_status.current_scope: Template and rejection rules for a future private incident tabletop and fake emergency patch rehearsal record only; it records no executed tabletop, no patch dry run, no signing or update dry run, no disclosure rehearsal, no postmortem, no role review, no backup coverage, and no owner approval.

rehearsal_lifecycle.required_record: Record the private report channel, named access roles, audit location, and restriction proof before any exploit details are handled.
Record the acknowledgement timestamp, private-channel preservation, minimal response text, and unsupported public promise boundaries.
Record fake vulnerability reproduction facts, affected commit or build candidate, platform, configuration, and sanitized artifact references.
Record asset impact, boundary crossed, reachability, prerequisites, confidence, and the owner who must review severity.
Record affected origin, profile, credential, update, signing, file, network, extension, DevTools, agent, commit, package, and channel scope.
Record embargo participants, evidence locations, expiry triggers, escalation path, and disclosure authority boundary.
Record protected branch settings, reviewer requirements, restricted visibility, and no self-approval policy.
Record embargoed CI commands, private logs, redaction checks, and failure handling without publishing exploit details.
Record regression evidence, backport decision, supported and unsupported branches, residual risk, and owner review status.
Record fake-key signing and update dry-run evidence, update metadata linkage, and explicit rejection of production keys or stable channels.
Record staged rollout cohort, pause and rollback decisions, minimum secure version, revocation behavior, and blocked public distribution.
Record draft release notes, user and admin communication, CVE and credit handling, coordinated disclosure timing, and legal/security review needs.
Record postmortem root cause, systemic fixes, tests, documentation updates, owners, follow-up deadlines, and closure blockers.
Record owner, reviewer, release, security, legal, support, on-call, backup coverage, timing targets, escalation, secret rotation, and hold or closure decision boundary.

rejection_rules.condition: Reject any rehearsal record that places exploitable vulnerability details, exploit payloads, credentials, secrets, or private-session data in public issue flow or public logs.
Reject records where an implementation agent decides vulnerability severity, disclosure timing, incident closure, stable promotion, signing authority, or supported security status.
Reject promotion when private report channel, access control, named access roles, audit trail, and confidential evidence location are missing.
Reject closure when private reproduction facts, affected commit or build, affected platform, configuration, and sanitized artifacts are missing.

Reject emergency patch capacity claims when protected branch, embargoed CI, regression, backport, signing/update dry run, staged rollout, minimum secure version, revocation, communication, coordinated disclosure, or postmortem evidence is missing.
Reject any rehearsal that uses production signing keys, offline root keys, stable channels, public update services, or public binary distribution.
Reject closure when regression evidence, backport decision, supported and unsupported branch scope, affected-version statement, or residual risk is absent.
Reject public notes or user and admin communication when security, legal, release, support, affected-version, mitigation, or disclosure approval is unresolved.
Reject promotion when owner, reviewer, release, security, legal, support, and on-call responsibilities, timing targets, escalation, and secret rotation are missing.
Reject incident-response readiness claims when backup-owner coverage, two-person control, or primary-only-path risk remains unresolved.
Reject incident closure authority, stable promotion authority, disclosure authority, signing authority, or emergency patch capacity based only on this template.
Reject supported security versions, production-safe browsing, incident-response readiness, release readiness, or production incident claims based only on fake tabletop records.
Reject evidence records that include unredacted exploit details, secrets, credentials, private-session data, raw user paths, or unsafe diagnostics.

unsupported_boundaries: no signing or update dry-run evidence
